ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag from vision pass; the depicted figure is a generic Duskmourn Nightmare creature (a phobia personified), not a named lore character. The Fear-of cycle is a design device, not a named NPC.

trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn: House of Horror (set code DSK) released 2024-09-27 as the 102nd Magic expansion and the second set in the Dragonstorm Arc. The plane is a single haunted house grown to swallow an entire world, ruled by the demon Valgavoth; Wizards designed the set top-down to homage 1970s-1990s slasher and haunted-house cinema. [Scryfall set page (dsk); MTG Wiki: Duskmourn: House of Horror, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: MTG Wiki: Duskmourn: House of Horror (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: The card belongs to the set's "Fear of..." Nightmare cycle: a group of uncommon Enchantment Creature - Nightmare cards each named after a specific phobia and embodying it as a stalking creature. Confirmed cycle members in the printed set (DSK) include Fear of Abduction (no. 9, white), Fear of Failed Tests (no. 55, blue), Fear of Falling (no. 56, blue), Fear of Isolation (no. 58, blue), Fear of Being Hunted (no. 134, red), Fear of Burning Alive (no. 135, red), and Fear of Missing Out (no. 136, red). All share the Enchantment Creature - Nightmare type-line and the "Fear of <phrase>" naming pattern. [Scryfall set DSK; per-card Scryfall pages via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Scryfall card pages for DSK-9, 55, 56, 58, 134, 135, 136 (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Wizards' Duskmourn Vision Design Handoff (Annie Sardelis) names suspense over shock as the set's core emotional thesis: "The director Alfred Hitchcock talked about the difference between suspense and shock and surprise. The former needs to inform you about a bad thing that could happen, forcing you to stew in your worries about it happening. Duskmourn wanted to be more about suspense than shock." The handoff also identifies enchantments as the card type best suited to expressing horror's mood because they have "a lingering presence on the board state" - which is why each phobia is rendered as an Enchantment Creature, not a regular creature. The Fear-of cycle is the most direct mechanical expression of that design call: the phobia stays in play, stalking. [Wizards: Duskmourn Vision Design Handoff Part 1, magic.wizards.com]
sources: Wizards: Duskmourn Vision Design Handoff Part 1 (magic.wizards.com/en/news/making-magic/duskmourn-house-of-horror-vision-design-handoff-part-1)

trivia_bullets.text: {1}{R}{R} for a 4/2 with Haste and "must be blocked if able" - the must-be-blocked clause is unusual in modern red and is the mechanical echo of the flavor: a stalker that does not hide, that demands engagement. The Haste + must-be-blocked pair functions as a forced-trade engine in aggressive red builds: the opponent cannot let a 4-power haster swing through, but a chump-block trades their creature for nothing. EDHREC rank 16,234 at retrieval (bulk-uncommon territory in Commander); Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, Commander legal. The preview source on Scryfall is LoadingReadyRun (2024-09-07). [Scryfall oracle_text, edhrec_rank, legalities, preview metadata]

Flavor text: "It makes no effort to hide itself, savoring its prey's rising panic as the sound of its clacking skull grows closer and closer." No attributed speaker - the line reads as omniscient narration of the phobia itself, which matches the cycle's design conceit that the named fear IS the creature. The audio detail ("clacking skull") and the explicit "makes no effort to hide" line up with the must-be-blocked rules clause: the monster announces itself. [Scryfall flavor_text]

Art by Maxime Minard (47 cards on Magic per the Gatherer artist index). Minard also painted Fear of Falling (DSK-56, the blue plummet-Nightmare) within the same Fear-of cycle, plus Unstoppable Slasher (DSK-119) and Creeping Peeper (DSK-46) elsewhere in the set's horror lineup. The visual through-line in his Duskmourn work is the gaunt, elongated silhouette and rim-lit predatory body language. [Scryfall artist field; Gatherer artist index: Maxime Minard, via WebSearch snippet; mtg.wtf artist page, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Gatherer: Maxime Minard artist index (via WebSearch snippet)

related_cards.name: Fear of Burning Alive
related_cards.note: Adjacent red Fear-of cycle member; with Fear of Missing Out (no. 136), the three reds occupy a continuous 134-135-136 run in the collector-number sequence. Already anchored to the fear-of-cycle character node in the corpus.

related_cards.name: Fear of Missing Out
related_cards.note: Third red Fear-of cycle member; completes the 134-135-136 red phobia run. Cycle roster expansion candidate.

related_cards.name: Fear of Abduction
related_cards.note: White Fear-of cycle member with Flying; the cycle's white slot. Already anchored to the fear-of-cycle character node in the corpus.

related_cards.name: Fear of Falling
related_cards.note: Blue Fear-of cycle member also by Maxime Minard - the artist-and-cycle double-connection makes this the closest sibling card.

related_cards.name: Fear of Failed Tests
related_cards.note: Blue Fear-of cycle member; Zimone Wola flavor-text speaker. Already anchored to the fear-of-cycle character node in the corpus.

related_cards.name: Fear of Isolation
related_cards.note: Blue Fear-of cycle member; expands the cycle roster beyond the five corpus-anchored members on the fear-of-cycle node. Candidate for orphan/archive write-up if a future bundle leans on the full cycle.

related_cards.name: Unstoppable Slasher
related_cards.note: Another Maxime Minard piece in DSK, in the same red stalker-horror visual register. Artist-cycle adjacency.
